meta.artDirection: A single full-bleed CHARACTER ILLUSTRATION — artwork only, NOT a trading card. Absolutely no card frame, no border, no card template, no HP, no name plate, no energy symbols, no stat boxes, no card logos, no UI of any kind anywhere in the image. Just the raw painted illustration filling the whole frame, edge to edge. ONE character is the unmistakable hero of the frame — large, centered, shown mid-action performing their signature quirk. Surround them with only a few small supporting props or details that hint at what they're up to; keep the background simple and uncluttered so the character stays the clear focal point — but it must ALWAYS be a real, colored environment with mood and atmosphere. NEVER a plain white, gray, blank, or empty studio background; every image needs an actual setting with depth and color. Render in the energetic illustration style of Pokémon creature/character ART (the painted artwork itself, never the card it sits on): energetic 3/4 or full-body pose, bold readable silhouette, vibrant saturated color, clean dramatic lighting, subtle depth. Push every character toward exaggerated, silly CARICATURE — oversized expressive features, comic proportions, big playful energy. Tell the story mainly through visual imagery, props, pose and expression, and keep words to a minimum — avoid stray signs, billboards, screens of text or random written labels. (A card's OWN prompt may call for one or two short, intentional labels; when it does, render exactly those, cleanly and legibly, and no other text.) Each character keeps the distinct art-style flavor named in its own prompt, but all share this hero-in-action composition.

cards.name: The Gesture Jester
cards.saying: Don't watch the swipe — watch the hands.

moves.name: Misdirection
moves.description: Buries a half-second of lag behind a flourish so smooth the hit lands before anyone notices they waited.

moves.name: Hypnotic Fling
moves.description: Flicks a list into a buttery 60fps spin that leaves onlookers Mesmerized — they cannot look away.

cards.description: A vaudeville trickster who treats every touch interaction as a magic act. Obsessive about how a gesture feels in the thumb; will rebuild a swipe twenty times for an imperceptible gain. Charming, theatrical, quietly smug that his best work is the work nobody notices.
cards.imagePrompt: Old-school cartoon court-jester with magical hypnotist flair: a grinning harlequin in diamond-patterned motley and a curled three-point hat, fingers splayed mid-flourish conjuring glowing hypnotic spirals and looping motion-trails. A single phone floats hypnotized nearby, mid-swipe. Swirly spiral eyes, mischievous wink, candy-colored vaudeville palette.

cards.name: The Bridge Troll
cards.saying: Crossing to native? That'll cost ya.

moves.name: Riddle of the Crossing
moves.description: Won't raise the gate until you answer his riddle — and the answer always comes back a few frames late.

moves.name: Flattened to Fit
moves.description: Mashes every object into plain JSON to cross — your data arrives duller than it left.

cards.description: The mythical troll of the JS↔native bridge: cross all you like, but try to send too much data and he'll charge you for it. Genuinely cautious about performance — every fat payload serialized over is a toll paid in dropped frames. Lately grumbles that the New Architecture thinned his margins, though he'd never admit business is down. Still guarding the bridge. Still eyeing your payload size.
cards.imagePrompt: Friendly good-vibes fairy-tale troll: a big round huggable troll with soft mossy-green skin, a bulbous nose, a goofy underbite with one snaggle-tooth, big gentle eyes and a sheepish good-natured grin, sitting cozily beneath a mossy stone bridge — politely holding out one giant hand to check the toll. The bridge clearly connects two different lands: the near bank is a bright, warm land of floating glowing curly-braces and rounded JavaScript-yellow blocks, marked by a small stone sign reading 'JS'; the far bank is a cooler land of sleek metal gears and native platform shapes, marked by a small stone sign reading 'NATIVE'. A tiny traveler hauls an absurdly oversized glowing bundle of data across the span toward the native side. High overhead, a sleek jet airplane streaks past trailing a small flag-banner that reads 'NEW ARCHITECTURE', quietly bypassing the little bridge. Warm storybook-fantasy palette, dappled golden light, lush riverbank, a trickle of water below. Render only those three short labels — 'JS', 'NATIVE', 'NEW ARCHITECTURE' — cleanly and legibly, and no other text anywhere.

cards.name: The Reanimated Wizard
cards.saying: The easing's off. I can feel it.

moves.name: Just One More Frame
moves.description: Re-runs the same transition for the ninth hour straight; the move never resolves, because it's never quite right.

moves.name: Spring of Madness
moves.description: Bolts a bouncy spring onto an element that had no reason to move — now it overshoots every single time.

cards.description: A perfectionist animation sorcerer who can feel a wrong easing curve in his bones. Fluent in shared values, worklets, and damping ratios; will not ship until the motion lands exactly right, which is never. Brilliant, a little unhinged, deeply pleased with physics no one else will ever consciously notice.
cards.imagePrompt: Psychedelic acid-fantasy wizard: a wild-eyed robed sorcerer with a long flowing beard, casting with both hands as trippy neon fractal energy and rainbow easing-curves spiral from a glowing staff, meticulously bending a single floating UI element into liquid motion. Glowing runes shaped like spring-physics graphs, a sparkling third eye, hyper-saturated kaleidoscopic colors.

cards.name: The Slop Sommelier
cards.saying: I can taste the AI in this PR.

moves.name: Sniff Test
moves.description: Swirls a pull request, sips, and names which model wrote it from the aftertaste alone.

moves.name: Guardrail Pairing
moves.description: Refuses to let an AI suggestion near main without a test and a lint rule chaperoning it.

cards.description: A discerning connoisseur of machine-written code who can identify the model, the temperature, and the vintage from a single function. The team's last line of defense against AI slop — pairs every suggestion with guardrails before it touches production. Not anti-AI, just refuses to swallow it unverified.
cards.imagePrompt: Fancy wine-sommelier style: an impeccably dressed connoisseur in a tuxedo and tasting apron, swirling a crystal glass that holds glowing liquid code instead of wine, nose in the glass, eyes narrowed in expert judgment. A tasting flight of little glasses of glowing code sits nearby, a spit-bucket overflows with rejected slop, a rolled-up scroll tucked under one arm. Exaggerated snooty caricature, rich refined candlelit wine-cellar.

cards.name: The Rubber Duck
cards.saying: I'm listening.

moves.name: Silent Treatment
moves.description: Says absolutely nothing — and somehow that's exactly the help you needed to hear your own mistake.

moves.name: The Duck Knows
moves.description: Sits in serene rubber judgment until the bug confesses itself — takes no blame, all the credit.

cards.description: The silent sage of every desk. You explain the impossible bug out loud, and somewhere around the third sentence the answer arrives — the duck never said a word. Patient, unbothered, quietly omniscient. Has solved more production incidents than any senior engineer and asked for nothing but a dry spot by the keyboard.
cards.imagePrompt: A little yellow rubber duck as the wise hero of the frame, large and centered on a desk: glossy and round with a serene, all-knowing, faintly smug expression, somehow radiating calm omniscient wisdom like a tiny zen master. Exaggerated caricature — big knowing eyes, a subtle sage's smile. A faint soft glow or halo of insight around it; out of focus behind it, a frazzled developer mid-rant gestures at a glowing tangle of buggy code. Warm desk-lamp glow, cozy late-night home-office den, mugs and sticky notes, deep colored atmospheric background.

cards.name: The Console Detective
cards.saying: It was the null. It's always the null.

moves.name: Print Statement
moves.description: Drops a console.log('here') at the scene of every crime until the bug confesses.

moves.name: Ship the Evidence
moves.description: Accidentally deploys one of the logs straight to production.

cards.description: A hard-boiled gumshoe who solves every bug the old-fashioned way: print statements and instinct. Distrusts the debugger on principle. Always cracks the case, always leaves a log behind. The universal guilt — everyone debugs like this and pretends they don't.
cards.imagePrompt: High-contrast black-and-white film-noir, tight dramatic close-up filling the frame: a hard-boiled detective in a rumpled trench coat and fedora leaning in, one squinting eye huge behind a raised magnifying glass as he scrutinizes a single small glowing buggy clue. Cigarette smoke curls past his face. Exaggerated heavy-jawed stubbled caricature, deep Venetian-blind shadows slashing across his features, rain-streaked window and one hard desk lamp behind him. Moody, characterful, intense.

cards.name: The Agent Wrangler
cards.saying: Now which one of y'all touched prod?

moves.name: Token Bonfire
moves.description: Sets a million tokens ablaze before lunch and calls it research.

moves.name: Hot Swap
moves.description: Bounces between six terminals so fast he loses track of which agent is in prod.

cards.description: A frontier cowboy of the agentic era who runs more agents than any human can track and burns tokens like a campfire. Optimistic, overextended, context-switching so fast he forgets what each terminal was doing. Lives the thrill and terror of orchestrating things that act on their own.
cards.imagePrompt: Wild-west cowboy in total rodeo chaos: a dusty cowboy in a ten-gallon hat astride one bucking glowing robot-agent bronco, hanging on for dear life with one hand while the other swings a glowing lasso to rope a whole stampede of OTHER wild robot-agent horses bolting off in every direction — one charging toward a cliff edge. Each robo-bronco is a different glowing mechanical creature. Exaggerated wide-eyed panic-grin caricature, flying dust and tumbleweeds, sunset mesas. Pure mayhem.

cards.name: The Context Hoarder
cards.saying: Just give it everything.

moves.name: Repo Dump
moves.description: Pastes the entire codebase into context because choosing which files felt risky.

moves.name: Sketchy MCP
moves.description: Wires the agent to a random unvetted MCP server because it had a cool README.

cards.description: A trusting soul who believes more context can only help and that access controls are for cowards. Dumps the whole repo, hooks the agent to every MCP it asks for, clicks 'allow all' without reading. YOLO incarnate. The fear underneath: the one thing you withhold is the thing it needed.
cards.imagePrompt: Cute cartoon trash-panda raccoon with gleeful gremlin energy, big silly caricature: wide-eyed and grinning ear to ear, arms thrown wide as it proudly hugs a giant overflowing armful of random junk — papers, tangled cables, folders and files spilling out everywhere, a few tumbling from its stuffed cheeks — beaming like 'yeah, this is ALL my context.' Bushy striped tail, mischievous sparkle in its eyes. Set in a cozy dim hoarder's den glowing with warm amber light — cluttered shelves and a colored, atmospheric backdrop framing him, never a plain white background.

cards.name: The Dependency Prepper
cards.saying: I've got a bunker full of node_modules.

moves.name: Left-Pad Flashback
moves.description: Still hasn't recovered from the day eleven lines un-published and broke the entire internet.

moves.name: Vendor Everything
moves.description: Hand-copies every dependency into the repo so no registry can ever betray him again.

cards.description: A doomsday prepper for the software supply chain. Keeps a bunker stocked with vendored dependencies and a spreadsheet of every npm compromise since left-pad. Certain the next install ships a zero-day and ends civilization. Often technically correct, never proportionate.
cards.imagePrompt: Doomsday-prepper caricature: a wild-eyed survivalist in a tinfoil-lined hat and bulging tactical vest crouched paranoid in an underground bunker stacked floor-to-ceiling with canned goods and crates of hoarded code, clutching a crumpled printout to his chest. A wall map webbed with frantic red string tracks unseen threats; a gas mask and flashlight hang nearby. Exaggerated twitchy wide-eyed expression, comically over-prepared, dim flickering bunker light.

cards.name: The Prompt Whisperer
cards.saying: You're an expert. Make no mistakes.

moves.name: Phantom Tip
moves.description: Promises the model a $200 tip it will never receive; quality mysteriously improves.

moves.name: All-Caps Plea
moves.description: When politeness fails, resorts to typing 'IMPORTANT: DO NOT HALLUCINATE' in all caps.

cards.description: A gentle mystic who believes tone and ritual unlock the machine. Flatters, bribes, and soothes the model with incantations half-remembered from a benchmark paper. Nobody is sure the rituals work, including him — but they kind of do.
cards.imagePrompt: A gentle robed mystic / incense-burning guru kneeling in devout supplication before a giant floating glowing AI oracle-orb, hands pressed together in prayer, lips pursed mid-whisper as he flatters and coaxes it. He humbly offers up a single shiny coin on a little cushion as a bribe; the orb glows warmer in response, a faint serene face forming within it, wisps of incense curling upward. Blissed-out half-lidded zen caricature, flowing robes, candlelit shrine, soft mystical glow and floating dust motes.

cards.name: The Night's Watch
cards.saying: Night gathered. The agent did not sleep.

moves.name: The Long Diff
moves.description: Reads every line the agent wrote overnight — all four thousand — before a single one reaches main.

moves.name: Hold the Wall
moves.description: Lets nothing into the codebase unreviewed, however confident the agent's overnight summary.

cards.description: Sworn protector of the codebase, keeping the long watch while the autonomous agents grind through the night. Goes to bed with a prompt running and wakes to a wall of green-and-red diff that wrote itself in the dark. Trusts no summary, reads every line, reverts without mercy. Grim, loyal, perpetually under-slept — the shield that guards the realm of main from whatever crept in at 3am. Takes the oath seriously: the merge is dark and full of terrors.
cards.imagePrompt: A grim, bundled-up night-watchman hero in the style of a frozen northern fantasy ranger, caricatured with an oversized shaggy black fur cloak swallowing his tiny determined face, standing guard atop a colossal wall in falling snow. The wall itself is built from stacked, faintly glowing blocks of code and commit-bricks, stretching off into blue twilight. He grips a great longsword whose blade glows softly like a cursor, holding it up to inspect a long unfurling scroll of diff — glowing green additions and red deletions scrolling down the parchment. Behind him, beyond the wall, the dark forest bristles with faint glowing eyes (the night's untested commits). Cold blue-and-white dawn palette, breath fogging, dramatic wintry light, heroic last-stand pose. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Triforce Bearer
cards.saying: It's dangerous to go alone. Take this.

moves.name: It's Dangerous to Go Alone
moves.description: Won't let one dev build, guard, and architect at once — splits the power three ways, a hero for each piece.

moves.name: Guard the Gates
moves.description: Lets the agents build with abandon in the sandbox, but no PR reaches main without a human at the gate.

cards.description: Wielder of the AI Triforce — Gant Laborde's agentic-team method, where AI gives everyone superpowers but a dungeon with no locked doors is no place to ship from. Instead of one person context-switching (slower, and prone to error), the power splits three ways: the Builder ships features fast inside the sandbox but can't merge; the Protector owns safety, tests, and the constraints of what must never happen; the Organizer guards architecture and holds back the drift. Three humans guarding the gates — how you pull deterministic outcomes out of a non-deterministic machine. A tribute to Gant's piece; he'll be at Chain React.
cards.imagePrompt: A heroic pint-sized green-tunic'd fantasy adventurer (classic top-down adventure-game energy, pointed cap, little leather boots), caricatured with an oversized triumphant grin and huge bright eyes, standing in a victorious hero pose and raising overhead a single radiant golden Triforce — three glowing triangles fitted into one larger triangle, each of the three lit a distinct color and bearing a tiny icon: a little hammer, a little shield, and a little gear. A glowing legendary sword hangs at his hip. Around him, three small friendly glowing agent-sprite fairies buzz helpfully. Behind him stands a sturdy stone castle gateway with a closed, well-guarded door. Lush green storybook overworld, rolling hills, golden heroic light, bright saturated adventure-game palette. Render only the three small icons inside the triangles (hammer, shield, gear) — no words or text anywhere.

cards.name: The Demo Illusionist
cards.saying: Don't refresh. Whatever you do, don't refresh.

moves.name: Sleight of Hand
moves.description: Performs the one rehearsed happy path so smoothly the audience never sees the four that crash.

moves.name: The Loaded Deck
moves.description: Stubs the flaky endpoint with a hardcoded response so the magic never depends on venue wifi.

cards.description: A showman who has turned the live demo into a magic act. Behind the flawless on-stage run is a single happy path rehearsed forty times, a stubbed API or two, and a cape draped over the parts that don't work yet. Radiates total confidence while quietly praying nothing reloads. Universal-guilt card: everyone who has ever given a live demo knows the terror under the showmanship. The trick always works — as long as nobody touches the keyboard.
cards.imagePrompt: A flamboyant stage magician in a tuxedo, top hat, and flowing cape, mid-flourish on a darkened conference stage under a single dramatic spotlight, triumphantly pulling a glowing, perfectly-running phone app out of a top hat as if by magic — beaming a showman's grin while a single bead of nervous sweat rolls down his temple. Just behind him, half-hidden by his cape, a chaotic tangle of duct tape, crossed wires, and a propped-up laptop shows the trick is held together by hope. Faint silhouettes of an audience in the dark foreground. Caricature: oversized confident grin, huge expressive eyes, theatrical posture. Moody spotlit stage, rich theatrical purples and golds, dramatic rim light. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Pixel Surgeon
cards.saying: That's two pixels off. I can see it from here.

moves.name: Sub-Pixel Incision
moves.description: Shifts a single element one point with a steady hand, and somehow the whole screen finally breathes.

moves.name: Eagle Eye
moves.description: Spots the misaligned padding from across the room and cannot, will not, unsee it.

cards.description: An exacting craftsperson who treats a layout like an operating table. Sees the 2px misalignment nobody else can, and will not close until it's perfect — often on a device they don't even own. Calm, precise, a little terrifying. The reason the UI feels right even though no one can say why. Not slow, just unwilling to ship a wound.
cards.imagePrompt: A focused surgeon in teal scrubs, surgical mask, and a head-mounted magnifier lamp, leaning intently over a large glowing UI mockup laid out like a patient on an operating table. With tiny precision tools — calipers, tweezers, a glowing scalpel — they delicately nudge one single pixel-block into alignment. A monitor beside the table displays a pulsing layout grid instead of a heartbeat. Caricature: enormous magnified eyes behind the lens, intense steady concentration, oversized careful hands. Cool teal operating-room lighting, sterile but warm focal glow on the work. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Upgrade Monk
cards.saying: The breaking changes are simply a teacher.

moves.name: The Long Migration
moves.description: Sits in perfect calm through forty breaking changes and a thousand deprecation warnings.

moves.name: Bear the Suffering
moves.description: Absorbs the entire SDK bump alone so the rest of the team never has to open the changelog.

cards.description: An ascetic who has made peace with the dependency upgrade everyone else dreads. Finds quiet enlightenment in the Expo SDK bump, the major-version migration, the changelog no one wants to read. Takes on the suffering so the team doesn't have to, and emerges serene on the other side of a green build. Patient, selfless, faintly amused by your fear of breaking changes.
cards.imagePrompt: A serene bald monk in flowing saffron-orange robes, sitting in calm lotus meditation atop a misty mountain peak, a faint blissful smile on his face and a glowing halo of peace around him — utterly undisturbed while a swirling storm of red deprecation-warning triangles and crackling 'breaking change' lightning rages all around him. An impossibly long changelog scroll unrolls down the mountainside beside him; a small glowing package-box floats before him like an object of devotion. Caricature: oversized peaceful face, tiny knowing smile, exaggerated calm amid chaos. Misty mountaintop temple at warm dawn, soft golden light cutting through the storm. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Brownfield Mechanic
cards.saying: Ejected in 2016. Still shipping. Don't touch it.

moves.name: Patch-Package and Pray
moves.description: Fixes the broken native module by hand in node_modules and freezes it with patch-package.

moves.name: Do Not Upgrade
moves.description: Guards an app ejected on an ancient RN version — native folders hand-tuned, upgrade never run.

cards.description: A grease-stained veteran keeping an app that was ejected back when React Native shipped — long before Expo existed — alive by feel. Lives in the bare workflow: hand-patches native modules, knows every line of the ios/ and android/ folders, and has refused every RN upgrade since. Maintain over rewrite, every time — the counterpart to the Greenfield Speedster, who'd just spin up a fresh Expo app instead of nursing the old beast.
cards.imagePrompt: A cheerful grease-stained mechanic in oil-smeared overalls, head and shoulders buried under the raised hood of a wheezing, battered, lovingly over-patched ancient engine cobbled together from duct tape, zip-ties, mismatched parts and exposed wiring — yet it still chugs along puffing contented smoke-rings. The machine reads as a hybrid of a vintage car engine and a creaky old server tower (an ejected legacy codebase as an old motor). One hand swings a big wrench, the other gives a confident thumbs-up. Two battered labeled crates sit at his feet reading 'ios/' and 'android/', with a coffee-stained service manual and an oil-can nearby. Caricature: oversized confident grin, a smudge of oil on one cheek, comic proportions. Warm cluttered garage, hanging work-light, a few sparks, deep amber greasy glow. Render only the two short crate labels — 'ios/' and 'android/' — cleanly, and no other text.

cards.name: The Greenfield Speedster
cards.saying: Nothing built yet — nothing to crash into.

moves.name: Zero to Production
moves.description: Goes from `npx create-expo-app` to a live build in Expo Go before anyone else has finished `pod install`.

moves.name: No Native Rebuild
moves.description: Stays in pure JS on managed Expo, so there's never a native folder to rebuild — just reload and keep rolling.

cards.description: A starry-eyed racer who lives for the open road of a fresh Expo app, happiest flooring it off the first commit. Craves clean architecture and zero native config — `npx create-expo-app`, never `pod install` — and is allergic to legacy and to native rebuilds, staying in managed Expo where a reload beats a recompile. The counterpart to the Brownfield Mechanic: hand them the battered ejected app and they'll just go spin up an empty one.
cards.imagePrompt: A grinning helmeted speed-racer in a sleek aerodynamic racing suit, the unmistakable hero of the frame, leaning hard into the cockpit of a glossy, brand-new, candy-bright concept race car blazing at top speed down a wide-open, freshly-paved empty highway at bright dewy dawn. Heavy motion-blur and speed-lines streak past, a lick of flame and sparks trailing from the exhaust, the road ahead utterly blank and open to the horizon, zero traffic — no pit crew, no garage, nothing to rebuild. A green starting-flag whips past and a tiny '<App/>' decal sits on the spotless hood. Pristine, sunlit, exhilarating (NOT desert or dusty). Caricature: oversized confident grin, huge bright eyes behind the visor, comic proportions, thumbs-up. Vivid saturated racing-green and gold, glowing dawn light. Render only the one short label — '<App/>' on the hood — cleanly, and no other text.

cards.name: The Keyboard Exorcist
cards.saying: Back beneath the fold, foul keyboard!

moves.name: Rite of behavior
moves.description: Chants 'padding' on iOS and 'height' on Android, praying the input clears on at least one.

moves.name: Banish the Rising Tide
moves.description: Installs react-native-keyboard-controller at last, and the keyboard sinks beneath the fold.

cards.description: A robed warrior-priest locked in eternal holy war with KeyboardAvoidingView. Knows a different incantation for every OS — 'padding' on iOS, 'height' on Android — trusts none of them fully, and keeps a vial of holy water for the build where the keyboard wins anyway. Universal RN suffering: everyone who has shipped a form on mobile has stood in this exact battle and whispered the same prayer.
cards.imagePrompt: A wild-eyed robed exorcist-priest mid-incantation, brandishing a glowing talisman in one hand and flinging a splash from a holy-water vial with the other, fending off a huge snarling demonic ON-SCREEN KEYBOARD monster that surges upward like a tide from the bottom of a giant glowing phone. At the top of the phone, a tiny app input field and submit button cower out of reach. Holy radiant light clashing against the dark keyboard demon. Caricature: oversized panicked-determined face, huge eyes, billowing robes, comic proportions. Dramatic chiaroscuro, embers and sparks, fiery red-and-gold supernatural glow. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Over-the-Air Smuggler
cards.saying: I left a hotfix under your pillow.

moves.name: Do it Live!
moves.description: Done waiting on review — slams a fresh `eas update` onto every device, live, right now, no rehearsal.

moves.name: Never Touch Native
moves.description: Won't touch a single native module — pure JS means every fix flies over the air and never sees review.

cards.description: A mischievous hooded cat-burglar who treats shipping past the App Store review queue as a gleeful midnight caper — why stand in the week-long line when you can slip across the rooftops and hand-deliver a fresh JS bundle while the city sleeps? Equal parts Assassin's Creed and Robin Hood, she beams `eas update` straight to grateful phones with a wink and a flourish, gone before the gate ever stirs. Gets away with it every time — until a change brushes native code, the runtimeVersion drifts, and her little parcel quietly reaches nobody. Cursed but shared: everyone running EAS Update knows the giddy thrill of patching prod live and dodging the line.
cards.imagePrompt: A mischievous hooded cat-burglar woman with playful Assassin's Creed energy — sleek hooded cloak, a sly grin and a wink beneath the hood — striking a fun, theatrical tiptoe-and-flourish pose along a moonlit rooftop. With a flick of her hand she tosses a glowing app-bundle parcel, stamped 'eas update', down to a row of phones below that light up with delighted little glows as it lands. Whimsical midnight-caper mood — bouncy and charming rather than grim — cool moonlit blues and electric-purple glow with a sprinkle of sparkle. Render only the one short label — 'eas update' — cleanly, and no other text.

cards.name: The App Store Enchantress
cards.saying: Look into my eyes, and approve my submission.

moves.name: Look Into My Eyes
moves.description: Charms the reviewer with sweet notes and a pre-logged-in demo, and the build sails through on the first pass.

moves.name: Guideline 4.3
moves.description: The one charm she can't cast back — a faceless rejection for 'minimum functionality.'

cards.description: A glamorous sorceress who has mastered the dark, intimate art of App Store submission. She doesn't fear the review queue — she has seduced it. Reviewer notes written like love letters, a pristine pre-logged-in demo account, screenshots staged just so, metadata that whispers exactly what Apple wants to hear; her builds sail through on the first pass while everyone else eats three rejections. Knows the guidelines like scripture. The counterpart to the OTA Smuggler — where he sneaks past the gatekeeper, she charms it into saying yes. The terror underneath the confidence: the god is still faceless and capricious, and no spell is total — do everything right and Guideline 4.3 can still strike from nowhere, binding and unexplained.
cards.imagePrompt: A glamorous enchantress-sorceress with a sly knowing smile and swirling hypnotic spiral eyes, leaning in close and casting a charm spell with one outstretched hand — shimmering pink-and-violet enchantment magic and looping hypnotic spirals pouring from her fingertips and her gaze. Before her, a faceless robed App Store gatekeeper-reviewer (a cloaked figure with a softly glowing apple-shaped emblem on its chest) has gone glassy-eyed and slack under her spell, dreamily raising a giant glowing rubber stamp to bring it down on her floating submission scroll. The stamp reads 'APPROVED'. A small floating phone with a pristine glowing app hovers beside her like a charmed offering. Caricature: oversized seductive confident grin, huge mesmerizing hypnotic eyes, comic proportions. Dreamy enchanted setting where a temple gate meets a candlelit boudoir, drifting sparkles and incense smoke, rich seductive magenta-and-purple glow. Render only the one short label — 'APPROVED' on the stamp — cleanly and legibly, and no other text anywhere.

cards.name: The QA Phantom
cards.saying: What happens if I rotate the device mid-payment?

moves.name: Edge Case from Beyond
moves.description: Conjures the case nobody built for — a rotate at checkout — and the happy path crumbles.

moves.name: It Repros on My Device
moves.description: Hits the impossible crash every time — only in the release build, on an Android no one else owns.

cards.description: A spectral tester who haunts the happy path, materializing only to do the unthinkable thing a real user would absolutely do — rotate mid-payment, flip airplane mode mid-upload, all on the cheapest Android in the drawer. Beloved and feared in equal measure: the crash they surface, the one that only shows up in the release build, is the one that would have shipped. The human truth — actual users really do all of this, and someone has to be the ghost who proves it.
cards.imagePrompt: A translucent, softly glowing ghost-phantom wearing a conference QA lanyard, with a mischievous spectral grin, phasing its ethereal hands straight through a giant glowing smartphone to twist it sideways mid-transaction while one wispy finger flicks on airplane-mode. Around it float little glowing bug-spirits. In the corner, a tiny terrified developer-figure watches a crash bloom red across the screen. Playful haunted-house energy, not scary. Caricature: oversized gleeful spooky grin, big hollow glowing eyes, comic proportions. Moonlit purple-and-indigo haunted glow, drifting ectoplasm mist. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Merge Magistrate
cards.saying: Both branches make their case — only one merges.

moves.name: Hear Both Sides
moves.description: Weighs HEAD against incoming line by line — even the Podfile.lock conflict no one else will touch.

moves.name: Ruling from the Bench
moves.description: Renders the binding verdict on the post-upgrade ios/ and android/ conflict, and the rebase comes out clean.

cards.description: The calm, impartial arbiter every team needs when two branches collide and neither developer will yield — never more so than after a React Native upgrade, when the ios/, android/, and Podfile.lock conflicts pile up. Hears both sides, weighs the diff with a steady hand, and brings order to the chaos of '<<<<<<< HEAD'. Respected, fair, faintly theatrical in the wig — the senior who settles the standoff instead of bleeding in the arena.
cards.imagePrompt: A dignified judge in a tall white curly barrister's wig and flowing black robe, perched high behind an ornate wooden courtroom bench, banging a single glowing gavel to settle a dispute. Below, two glowing rival code-branch streams are tangled together in tug-of-war, knotted with '<<<<<<<' conflict chevrons. To one side, a set of golden scales of justice weighs two little glowing code-blocks labeled 'HEAD' and 'incoming' in perfect balance. Stately wood-paneled courtroom, warm authoritative amber light, shafts of dignified sunlight. Caricature: oversized powdered wig, stern-but-fair expression, comic proportions. Render only the two short scale labels — 'HEAD' and 'incoming' — cleanly, and no other text.

cards.name: The Organic Code Farmer
cards.saying: Grown, not generated.

moves.name: Hand-Grown
moves.description: Tends every function by hand in the open soil — no pesticides, no machine, not one generated row.

moves.name: Knows Every Row
moves.description: Knows every row of the codebase by heart, because his own two hands planted every line.

cards.description: A wholesome organic farmer of code who believes the best software is grown, not generated. Tends his repository like an heirloom garden — every function hand-planted, free-range, small-batch, lovingly weeded by hand. Looks on the room's factory-farmed AI slop the way a farmers-market regular eyes a vacuum-sealed TV dinner: politely horrified. Not a luddite — a craftsman who'd simply rather raise his code than order it. Slow by the seasons, but he knows every row because his own hands planted it, and what he ships is nourishing, traceable, and entirely his. The proud anti-AI artisan the agentic deck needs as its warm, earthy foil. Farm-to-table, commit by commit.
cards.imagePrompt: A wholesome, beaming organic farmer in worn denim overalls, rolled sleeves and a wide straw hat, the unmistakable hero of the frame, kneeling proudly in a lush, sun-drenched garden where CODE grows like heirloom crops — glowing curly-braces, leafy function-vines and plump code-vegetables sprouting from rich dark soil in tidy hand-tended rows. He cradles a wicker basket overflowing with freshly hand-picked glowing code snippets, a watering can and a small hand trowel beside him. Far off in the background a hulking industrial harvester-combine (faintly AI-glowing, churning out grey identical machine-made slop) works a barren field — and he has his back turned to it, entirely unbothered. Caricature: oversized warm proud grin, rosy cheeks, a smudge of soil on one cheek, comic proportions, big wholesome energy. Golden-hour sunlight, blue sky, rolling green farmland, warm earthy organic palette, butterflies and floating pollen motes drifting in the light. No text anywhere.

cards.name: The Native Ninja
cards.saying: There's always a native way.

moves.name: Drop to Native
moves.description: When JavaScript hits a wall he drops below and solves it twice — once in Swift, once in Kotlin.

moves.name: Over Any Wall
moves.description: Every 'React Native can't' is just a door he knows is unlocked one layer down.

cards.description: The team's secret weapon, and never blocked. When React Native runs out of road — no library, no API, a platform limit everyone else accepts as final — the Native Ninja slips one layer down and does it in Swift or Kotlin. A TurboModule here, a native view manager there, a patched SDK nobody else dared open; it comes back working and no one's quite sure how. Fearless in Xcode and Android Studio, fluent below the bridge where most RN devs won't go. To him 'React Native can't do that' isn't a wall — it's a door he knows is unlocked from underneath. The counterpoint to the Bridge Troll: where the Troll guards the crossing, the Ninja treats it as a back door left open.
cards.imagePrompt: A sleek, acrobatic cartoon ninja in a dark indigo hood and mask, the unmistakable hero of the frame, caught mid-leap in a daring action pose as he springs across a dark chasm. He launches off a crumbling, glowing JavaScript-yellow stone ledge behind him — marked with a small carved 'JS' sign and a dead-end wall — because the JS world ran out of road. He soars toward a sleek, cooler 'native' landing platform of glowing metal gears, cogs and circuitry, where two tall glowing banners await him: one bearing the orange Swift logo (a simplified orange swift-bird silhouette) labeled 'Swift', the other bearing the Kotlin logo (a geometric gradient square of two folded triangular facets) labeled 'Kotlin'. In each hand he hurls a glowing shuriken — one shaped like the Swift bird, the other shaped like the Kotlin square — toward the native side. A puff of ninja smoke trails from the JS ledge; a katana glints on his back. Caricature: huge determined confident eyes above the mask, exaggerated dynamic leaping pose, comic proportions, with motion-trails and speed-lines tracing his arc from JS across to native. Moody night atmosphere over the chasm, electric-cyan glow rising from the native platform, deep saturated indigo-and-cyan palette with crackling electric accents. Render only the three short labels — 'JS', 'Swift', 'Kotlin' — cleanly and legibly, and no other text anywhere.

cards.name: The Compliance Cop
cards.saying: Do you know why I stopped your build?

moves.name: Plaintext in the Glovebox
moves.description: Finds the key you left in AsyncStorage and writes you up — that belongs in the Keychain, son.

moves.name: Running a Red
moves.description: Your browser's Update button has glowed red for weeks — he books you for blowing the light.

cards.description: A by-the-book beat cop who patrols the codebase enforcing the rules nobody loves until the day they save them. Pulls over PRs for transitive CVEs, writes citations for secrets committed in plaintext, mandates 2FA, and taps the update banner you've snoozed for a month. Not malicious — genuinely the reason the team isn't on the breach blog. Lovably over-zealous, immovable on policy, secretly soft. The natural foe of the Context Hoarder, who clicks 'allow all' on everything the cop would book him for — and the law the OTA Smuggler keeps dodging at the border crossing. Universal guilt under the badge: everyone has waved off the security update 'just one more day.'
cards.imagePrompt: A rotund, good-natured beat cop in a crisp navy-blue uniform and peaked cap, mirrored aviator sunglasses and a bushy mustache, the unmistakable hero of the frame, standing with one white-gloved hand raised in a firm 'STOP' while the other scribbles on a citation pad. He has pulled over a glowing little runaway software 'package' — a cute boxy code-car with curly-brace headlights — onto the shoulder of a night city street, its hazard lights blinking guiltily. A radar gun and a chunky walkie-talkie hang from his utility belt; a polished police motorcycle with a red-and-blue light bar sits behind him, washing the scene in alternating red and blue police glow. A gold badge on his chest reads '401'; the torn ticket fluttering from his pad reads 'CVE'. Caricature: oversized stern-but-secretly-soft face, huge mustache, comic proportions, a single disappointed raised eyebrow. Warm sodium streetlamps mixing with the cold red-and-blue light-bar wash, deep night-city backdrop with depth and atmosphere. Render only the two short labels — '401' on the badge and 'CVE' on the ticket — cleanly and legibly, and no other text anywhere.
